pull.title: [Bugfix] Fix CUDA compatibility path setting for both datacenter and consumer NVIDIA GPUs
pull.body:
## Purpose

Fix #32373
Fix #33369
Closes #34226

Fixes the core problem in https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/32373 and https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/33369, introduced from https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/30784 and https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/33116.

The issue with the CUDA compatibility path setting for both datacenter and consumer NVIDIA GPUs:
(1) Consumer NVIDIA GPUs must NOT have the CUDA compatibility libraries inside `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`.
(2) Professional and Datacenter NVIDIA GPUs support CUDA compatibility libraries in certain cases, and should be enabled selectively.

Anything else than editing the vLLM executable is not intuitive, and the only alternative is for the user to manually edit the `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`. Currently, all GeForce GPU users are experiencing errors with the vllm/vllm-openai container.

## Test Plan

Edit: created in `tests/cuda/test_cuda_compatibility_path.py` thanks to @88plug.

```shell
pytest tests/cuda/test_cuda_compatibility_path.py -v
```

21 unit tests covering:

* Env var parsing (`0`/`1`/`true`/`false`/whitespace variants)
* Path detection priority (custom override > conda > default `/usr/local/cuda-{ver}/compat`)
* `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` prepend, deduplication, and no-op when already at front
* Graceful handling when no valid compat path exists
* `_get_torch_cuda_version()` with and without torch available

## Test Result
```
21 passed in 1.25s
```

## Legacy Test Plan

With `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1` and e.g., `VLLM_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY_PATH=/usr/local/cuda-12.9/compat/` configured, **and the Python files in this current PR patched**, run any vLLM entrypoint, e.g.,

```bash
python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model <model_name> --tensor-parallel-size 2 --trust-remote-code --max-num-seqs 16
```

Instructions to build and test #33992 (change max_jobs and nvcc_threads as appropriate):

Build only the wheel from an existing container (Add `-v ~/vllm:/workspace/vllm` if `~/vllm` is the result of `git clone -b pr https://github.com/ehfd/vllm.git`):
```bash
git clone -b pr https://github.com/ehfd/vllm.git
cd vllm
# Change cu129 to cu130 when inside CUDA 13.0 container
export VLLM_PRECOMPILED_WHEEL_VARIANT="cu129"
export VLLM_PRECOMPILED_WHEEL_COMMIT="342a7cda2d212205c4874f82a59559400bbec311"
export VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1
uv pip install --system --editable .
export VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1
# Change 12.9 to 13.0 when inside CUDA 13.0 container
export VLLM_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY_PATH="/usr/local/cuda-12.9/compat"
(Commands to invoke vLLM)
```

Running:

For datacenter/professional GPUs: Set `-e VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1` for e.g. NVIDIA 575 + CUDA 13.0 / Set `-e VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0` for e.g. NVIDIA 580 + CUDA 13.0.
For consumer GPUs: Set `-e VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0` at all times.

```bash
docker run -it --entrypoint "" --runtime nvidia --gpus all \
    -v ~/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface \
    -p 5000:5000 \
    --env "HF_TOKEN=<secret>" \
    vllm/vllm-openai:nightly /bin/bash

# Change cu129 to cu130 when inside CUDA 13.0 container
$ export VLLM_PRECOMPILED_WHEEL_VARIANT="cu129"
$ export VLLM_PRECOMPILED_WHEEL_COMMIT="342a7cda2d212205c4874f82a59559400bbec311"
$ export VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1
$ uv pip install --system --editable .
```

## Test Result

1x NVIDIA A100 with NVIDIA 575.57.08 + CUDA 12.9 with `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0`:
```
$ rm -f /etc/ld.so.conf.d/cuda-compat.conf
$ ldconfig
$ ldconfig -p | grep libcuda
        libcudart.so.12 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so.12
        libcudart.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so
        libcudadebugger.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcudadebugger.so.1
        libcuda.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so.1
        libcuda.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so
$ export VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0
$ python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --port "5000" --host 0.0.0.0 --download-dir /workspace/.cache/huggingface/hub --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B --served-model-name qwen3 --reasoning-parser deepseek_r1 --tensor-parallel-size "1" --trust-remote-code

Logs:
Application startup complete.
```

1x NVIDIA A100 with NVIDIA 575.57.08 + CUDA 12.9 with `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1`:
```
$ rm -f /etc/ld.so.conf.d/cuda-compat.conf
$ ldconfig
$ ldconfig -p | grep libcuda
        libcudart.so.12 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so.12
        libcudart.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so
        libcudadebugger.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcudadebugger.so.1
        libcuda.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so.1
        libcuda.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so
$ export VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1
$ python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --port "5000" --host 0.0.0.0 --download-dir /workspace/.cache/huggingface/hub --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B --served-model-name qwen3 --reasoning-parser deepseek_r1 --tensor-parallel-size "1" --trust-remote-code

Logs:
Application startup complete.
```

1x NVIDIA A100 with NVIDIA 575.57.08 + CUDA 13.0 with `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0` (Expected Result):
```
$ rm -f /etc/ld.so.conf.d/cuda-compat.conf
$ ldconfig
$ ldconfig -p | grep libcuda
        libcudart.so.13 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so.13
        libcudart.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so
        libcudadebugger.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcudadebugger.so.1
        libcuda.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so.1
        libcuda.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so
$ export VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0
$ python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --port "5000" --host 0.0.0.0 --download-dir /workspace/.cache/huggingface/hub --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B --served-model-name qwen3 --reasoning-parser deepseek_r1 --tensor-parallel-size "1" --trust-remote-code

Logs:
RuntimeError: The NVIDIA driver on your system is too old (found version 12090). Please update your GPU driver by downloading and installing a new version from the URL: http://www.nvidia.com/Download/index.aspx Alternatively, go to: https://pytorch.org to install a PyTorch version that has been compiled with your version of the CUDA driver.
```

1x NVIDIA A100 with NVIDIA 575.57.08 + CUDA 13.0 with `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1`:
```
$ rm -f /etc/ld.so.conf.d/cuda-compat.conf
$ ldconfig
$ ldconfig -p | grep libcuda
        libcudart.so.13 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so.13
        libcudart.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so
        libcudadebugger.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcudadebugger.so.1
        libcuda.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so.1
        libcuda.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so
$ export VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1
$ python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --port "5000" --host 0.0.0.0 --download-dir /workspace/.cache/huggingface/hub --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B --served-model-name qwen3 --reasoning-parser deepseek_r1 --tensor-parallel-size "1" --trust-remote-code

Logs:
Application startup complete.
```

1x NVIDIA Quadro RTX 6000 with NVIDIA 580.126.09 + CUDA 12.9 with `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0`:
```
$ rm -f /etc/ld.so.conf.d/cuda-compat.conf
$ ldconfig
$ ldconfig -p | grep libcuda
        libcudart.so.12 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so.12
        libcudart.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so
        libcudadebugger.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcudadebugger.so.1
        libcuda.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so.1
        libcuda.so (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so
$ export VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0
$ python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --port "5000" --host 0.0.0.0 --download-dir /workspace/.cache/huggingface/hub --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B --served-model-name qwen3 --reasoning-parser deepseek_r1 --tensor-parallel-size "1" --trust-remote-code

Logs:
Application startup complete.
```

1x NVIDIA Quadro RTX 6000 with NVIDIA 580.126.09 + CUDA 12.9 with `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1`:
(Expected Result, means that CUDA compatibility library is successfully prioritized when `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1`and not set when `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0`)
```
$ rm -f /etc/ld.so.conf.d/cuda-compat.conf
$ ldconfig
$ ldconfig -p | grep libcuda
        libcudart.so.12 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so.12
        libcudart.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so
        libcudadebugger.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcudadebugger.so.1
        libcuda.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so.1
        libcuda.so (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so
$ export VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1
$ python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --port "5000" --host 0.0.0.0 --download-dir /workspace/.cache/huggingface/hub --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B --served-model-name qwen3 --reasoning-parser deepseek_r1 --tensor-parallel-size "1" --trust-remote-code

Logs:
RuntimeError: Unexpected error from cudaGetDeviceCount(). Did you run some cuda functions before calling NumCudaDevices() that might have already set an error? Error 803: system has unsupported display driver / cuda driver combination
```

1x NVIDIA Quadro RTX 6000 with NVIDIA 580.126.09 + CUDA 13.0 with `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0`:

```
$ rm -f /etc/ld.so.conf.d/cuda-compat.conf
$ ldconfig
$ ldconfig -p | grep libcuda
        libcudart.so.13 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so.13
        libcudart.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so
        libcudadebugger.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcudadebugger.so.1
        libcuda.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so.1
        libcuda.so (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so
$ export VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0
$ python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --port "5000" --host 0.0.0.0 --download-dir /workspace/.cache/huggingface/hub --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B --served-model-name qwen3 --reasoning-parser deepseek_r1 --tensor-parallel-size "1" --trust-remote-code

Logs:
Application startup complete.
```

1x NVIDIA Quadro RTX 6000 with NVIDIA 580.126.09 + CUDA 13.0 with `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1`:
(Expected Result, means that CUDA compatibility library is successfully prioritized when `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1`and not set when `VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=0`)
```
$ rm -f /etc/ld.so.conf.d/cuda-compat.conf
$ ldconfig
$ ldconfig -p | grep libcuda
        libcudart.so.13 (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so.13
        libcudart.so (libc6,x86-64) => /usr/local/cuda/targets/x86_64-linux/lib/libcudart.so
        libcudadebugger.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcudadebugger.so.1
        libcuda.so.1 (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so.1
        libcuda.so (libc6,x86-64) => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcuda.so
$ export VLLM_ENABLE_CUDA_COMPATIBILITY=1
$ python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --port "5000" --host 0.0.0.0 --download-dir /workspace/.cache/huggingface/hub --model Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B --served-model-name qwen3 --reasoning-parser deepseek_r1 --tensor-parallel-size "1" --trust-remote-code

Logs:
RuntimeError: Unexpected error from cudaGetDeviceCount(). Did you run some cuda functions before calling NumCudaDevices() that might have already set an error? Error 803: system has unsupported display driver / cuda driver combination
```

---
<details>
<summary> Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist </summary>

- [x] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)".
- [x] The test plan, such as providing test command.
- [x] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results
- [x] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model.
- [ ] (Optional) Release notes update. If your change is user facing, please update the release notes draft in the [Google Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YyVqrgX4gHTtrstbq8oWUImOyPCKSGnJ7xtTpmXzlRs/edit?tab=t.0).
</details>

labels.name: ready
labels.description: ONLY add when PR is ready to merge/full CI is needed

repo.name: vllm
repo.description: A high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for LLMs
